summary:
Microsoft’s Azure ecosystem updates were dominated by agentic AI and operational tooling: Microsoft announced Foundry Local is now generally available, published patterns for building and monitoring multi-agent apps on Azure App Service with Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0, and detailed internal use of an Azure SRE Agent with agentic workflows; related posts also covered agentic function-calling, AI observability via OpenTelemetry and the new Application Insights Agents View, and agentic enhancements in the Azure NetApp Files VS Code Extension 1.2.0. Security was the other major theme, with Microsoft outlining its vision for an “agentic SOC,” adding Ask Copilot to security assessments, sharing threat research on Storm-2755 and an Android wallet SDK vulnerability, and publishing deep technical guidance on sovereignty in Azure Belgium Central, enterprise UAMI trust boundaries, Azure Policy deployment of Ansible playbooks through Azure Arc Machine Configuration private preview, and end-to-end MLOps pipelines to managed endpoints.

Secondary updates included developer productivity improvements across GitHub—notification sorting, lower-quality comment hiding, PR role labels, release info in the issue sidebar, and default project field values—plus infrastructure and app-platform items like running multiple Aspire AppHost instances without port conflicts and the Terraform v1.15.0-rc1 release.
